text: Planning a trip can be both exciting and stressful. Before my journey to Japan, I spent weeks researching flights, accommodation, and must-see attractions. When I arrived, everything felt new and different. I loved exploring the local culture, from traditional tea ceremonies to busy city streets. The food was a highlight—I tried sushi, ramen, and street snacks that I had never tasted before. However, language barriers sometimes made things tricky, like ordering at a restaurant or asking for directions. Despite these small challenges, the experience was unforgettable. Now, the memories of that adventure—like watching cherry blossoms fall—still bring a smile to my face.
